templates.description: User reports being unable to connect to the corporate Wi-Fi from their laptop on the {floor} floor.
correct_steps: Verify SSID and credentials with user
Forget and re-add the Wi-Fi profile
Reset the network adapter
Reconnect and confirm connectivity
templates.rationale: Single-user Wi-Fi failure is a standard Tier 1 fix: re-add profile and reset adapter. No infrastructure change is required.
templates.outage_description: Multiple users on the {floor} floor report total Wi-Fi loss.

templates.description: Remote user states the corporate VPN drops repeatedly during the day.
correct_steps: Confirm VPN client version is current
Re-import the VPN profile
Test connection on a wired link to rule out Wi-Fi
Document stability before closing
templates.rationale: Intermittent VPN drops for one user are usually a stale client/profile. Tier 1 reinstall + retest before involving Network Ops.

templates.description: Users report extremely slow internet in the main conference room during meetings.
correct_steps: Run a speed test from a room device
Capture results, time, and SSID/AP MAC
Open Tier 2 ticket with evidence and AP name
templates.rationale: Localized slowness affecting many users points to AP saturation or uplink — Tier 2 must look at controller stats. Tier 1 collects evidence and hands off.

templates.short_description: Intermittent packet loss to data center
templates.description: Application teams report 2-5% packet loss to the primary DC during peak hours; affects multiple business units.
correct_steps: Collect MTR/traceroute from at least 3 source segments
Pull interface error counters from edge switches
Correlate with change management for any recent routing changes
Open P2 with Tier 3 referencing CR ticket numbers
templates.rationale: Cross-segment packet loss is a routing/transit issue. Tier 3 needs MTRs and interface stats from multiple vantage points before they can act.

templates.description: User states their company laptop is unresponsive after a weekend.
correct_steps: Verify the power adapter and outlet
Perform a 30-second hard reset (battery drain)
Boot to BIOS to confirm hardware health
Document boot result
templates.rationale: Unresponsive laptop is a textbook Tier 1 walk-through: power source, hard reset, BIOS check before any hardware swap.

templates.description: External monitor flickers intermittently when the user moves the cable.
correct_steps: Reseat the display cable
Swap to a known-good cable
Test on a second port
Replace monitor only if cable swaps fail
templates.rationale: Flicker that follows cable movement is almost always a cable/connector issue. Cheap swap first, replace last.

templates.description: Department printer is online but jobs sit in the queue.
correct_steps: Clear the print queue on the user's machine
Restart the print spooler service
Send a test page
Confirm with user
templates.rationale: Stuck queue + online printer = client-side spooler. Tier 1 fix in under 5 minutes.
templates.outage_description: Several department printers are offline and not accepting print jobs.

templates.short_description: Docking station not detecting external displays
templates.description: User's Thunderbolt dock will not light up either external monitor after a recent OS update.
correct_steps: Update the dock firmware to the latest vendor release
Reinstall Thunderbolt and graphics drivers
Power-cycle the dock (unplug 30s)
Verify both displays after reboot
templates.rationale: Post-update dock failures usually need vendor firmware + driver refresh. Beyond Tier 1's standard image, but still resolvable at Tier 2.

templates.short_description: Recurring blue screen on engineering workstations
templates.description: Three engineering workstations have BSOD'd with DPC_WATCHDOG_VIOLATION in the past 48 hours after a GPU driver push.
correct_steps: Collect minidumps from all affected machines
Identify common driver versions and recent SCCM packages
Roll back the GPU driver on one machine as a controlled test
Open a P2 with Endpoint Engineering including dump analysis
templates.rationale: A reproducible BSOD code across multiple machines after a driver push is a fleet-level issue. Tier 3 owns the package; Tier 1/2 collects evidence.

templates.description: User is locked out of their corporate account after multiple failed attempts.
correct_steps: Verify identity using two pieces of information
Reset password in Active Directory
Force change at next login
Confirm successful login with user
templates.rationale: Identity verification + AD reset is the canonical Tier 1 task. Always force change at next login.

templates.description: User cannot open the Finance shared drive after a recent department transfer.
correct_steps: Confirm the user's current department in HR system
Check current AD group membership
Add user to the correct AD security group
Have user sign out and back in, confirm access
templates.rationale: Department transfers commonly miss group migration. Tier 1 verifies + adds to the correct group.

templates.description: User reports losing their MFA hardware token while traveling.
correct_steps: Verify identity via a secondary channel (manager callback)
Place a hold on the account
Open a Sec Ops ticket for token revocation and reissue
templates.rationale: A lost MFA token is a security event, not a self-service reset. Identity is verified out-of-band; Sec Ops revokes and reissues.

templates.short_description: Service account authentication failures across multiple apps
templates.description: An application service account is failing Kerberos auth against several internal apps after a domain controller change.
correct_steps: Collect klist and event log entries from app servers
Confirm SPN registration on the service account
Identify recent DC or schema changes via change management
Escalate to Identity Engineering with evidence and timeline
templates.rationale: Cross-app Kerberos failure tied to a DC change is an infrastructure issue. Tier 3 (Identity Engineering) owns SPN/DC; lower tiers gather evidence.

templates.description: Outlook closes unexpectedly after the user opens certain emails.
correct_steps: Start Outlook in safe mode
Disable add-ins one at a time
Recreate the mail profile if needed
Confirm stability with user
templates.rationale: Outlook crashes are typically add-in or profile corruption. Standard Tier 1 isolation.

templates.description: User requests installation of approved design software on their workstation.
correct_steps: Verify license entitlement and manager approval
Push the package via SCCM/Intune
Confirm install and license activation with user
templates.rationale: Approved-software install is a Tier 1 fulfillment task once entitlement is verified.

templates.description: User cannot submit purchase orders, gets HTTP 500 in the ERP web client.
correct_steps: Capture an error screenshot and HAR file
Note exact time and user ID for log correlation
Confirm scope (other users, other modules)
Escalate to ERP Application Team with evidence
templates.rationale: HTTP 500 means a server-side failure — Tier 1/2 cannot fix it. The job is to collect evidence so the app team can diagnose.
templates.outage_description: Multiple users cannot access the ERP application; error 500 on submit.

templates.short_description: Database query timeouts in production reporting app
templates.description: Reporting app is timing out on queries that ran in seconds last week. Affects month-end close.
correct_steps: Capture exact query, timeout value, and start time
Pull execution plan and recent index/stats changes
Check for blocking sessions and tempdb pressure
Open a P1 to DBA with evidence; loop in app team
templates.rationale: Sudden timeout regression is a database performance issue (stats, plan, blocking). DBAs own the tuning; everyone else collects evidence.
